extraction.one_line_summary: HAYTER Ethan won a reduced sprint after Ineos Grenadiers' DE PLUS set infernal tempo on late drag that dropped 70+ riders including sprinter VERNON
tactical_patterns: Ineos Grenadiers used DE PLUS tempo on undulating drags to shed weak sprinters before leadout, reducing bunch to 70 riders.
Magnus SHEFFIELD executed exceptionally long leadout allowing HAYTER to open sprint very early and hold off closers on technical finish.
UAE Emirates' POLANC did extended chase work despite no sprinter, suggesting GC positioning priority on supposedly non-decisive stage.
Alexandr VLASOV finished best among GC riders by following sprint, revealing his ability to contest reduced bunch sprints after selective racing.

rider_signals.VERNON Ethan: negative: dropped on late drag despite Quick-Step support, exposing climbing weakness that prevents contesting reduced sprints
rider_signals.HAYTER Ethan: positive: won from very early sprint launch after selective racing, demonstrating both climbing durability and confidence in power
rider_signals.SHEFFIELD Magnus: positive: executed exceptionally long leadout suggesting diesel engine that can set sprint from distance on punchy finales
rider_signals.VLASOV Alexandr: positive: best GC finisher by actively following sprint rather than soft-pedaling, showing opportunistic positioning instincts
rider_signals.DE PLUS Laurens: positive: set pace that shredded peloton on non-categorized drag, revealing Ineos depth for controlling punchy stages
